headline: 'A more arbitrary and extreme regime': In the West Bank, Israel toughens death penalty law for Palestinians
description: A coalition of Israeli and Palestinian non-profit organizations has called for the cancellation of the 'military order' that brought the measure into force, expanding the crimes punishable by death and reducing the means of defense.

headline: At the G7, the US wants no mention of the words 'climate change'
Former Arafat adviser faces deportation from France
'Heat strokes are part of the job': In Delhi, construction workers endure 43°C heat
description: India's capital has faced scorching days since April. On construction sites, workers have tried to adapt but many have fallen victim to heatstroke.

headline: Historic buildings in Normandy's Honfleur on verge of collapse
description: Along the port city's old harbor, two evacuated buildings threaten to bring down others with them. Traffic is banned, and 19 businesses have shuttered. The lack of maintenance and alterations made by owners of seasonal rentals are being blamed.

headline: Jeremy D. Popkin, historian: 'Debates on slavery began decades before the French Revolution'
description: The American scholar traces the legal and intellectual history of abolitionism in France, showing how the question of slavery influenced the trajectories of many leading figures of the revolutionary movement.

headline: 'France's president-PM diarchy is shaping the strategy of many 2027 hopefuls'
description: The role of prime minister has regained central importance since 2022 and the end of outright majorities in the Assemblée Nationale, observes Le Monde columnist Françoise Fressoz.

EU approves trade deal with Washington and sets safeguards

Trump sinks lower into nepotism with fund to compensate allies
description: Acting attorney general Todd Blanche announced the creation of a fund totaling $1.776 billion for Trump's prosecuted allies and established a 'permanent' ban on tax audits into the president and his family.

headline: In a tsunami of data leaks, French society remains vulnerable and authorities powerless
description: France is experiencing a record number of data breaches. Despite the damage caused by these hacks, the level of cybersecurity in businesses and government agencies remains inadequate.

headline: As Israeli elections approach, the opposition focuses on rejecting Netanyahu rather than defining peace
description: Even as the current prime minister's coalition appears to be in deep trouble, most of his rivals have not dared to break with his nationalist rhetoric, and seem trapped in a conceptual dead end when it comes to coexistence with Palestinians – whether Israeli citizens, residents of Gaza, or the West Bank.
